ab: te mai na teko hia uhne teova?
en: How do you go to the block-house?

ab: te’o yonto kuyai ho uhne teova.
en: I drive to the block-house.

ab: te mai na teta hia uhne veoing?
en: How does he go to the hospital?

ab: teta coeconʉ ho uhne veoing.
en: He walks to the hospital.

ab: te mai na teko hia uhne lanyi?
en: How do you go to Penghu?

ab: la’u yonto hikooki ho uhne lanyi.
en: I go to Penghu by plane.

ab: te mai na teko hia uhne lanyi?
en: How do you go to Penghu?

ab: la’u yonto ’apangu ho uhne lanyi.
en: I go to Penghu by boat.

ab: teko yono cuma ho maine’e?
en: How do you go home?

ab: te’o nooyo to kuyai no mayahe.
en: I go home by MRT.

ab: te yono cuma ’o apu’u ho te uhne ’oyonatmopsʉ?
en: How does apu’u go to school?

ab: te nooyo to kuyai no yaapuzu ’o apu’u ho uhne ’oyonatmopsʉ.
en: apu’u goes to school by train.

ab: te yono cuma ’o pasuya ho te uhne kiokai?
en: How does pasuya go to church?

ab: te yonto zitensa ’o pasuya ho uhne kiokai.
en: pasuya goes to church by bicycle.

ab: lako yonto kuyai no mayahe ho uhne ’oyonatmopsʉ?
en: Do you go to school by MRT?

ab: o’a, mi’o nooyo to vasu ho uhne ’oyonatmopsʉ.
en: No, I go to school by bus.

ab: lako yonto zitensa ho uhne ’oyonatmopsʉ?
en: Do you go to school by bicycle?

ab: teno, la’u yonto zitensa ho uhne ’oyonatmopsʉ.
en: Yes, I go to school by bicycle.

ab: lako nooyo to kaothe ho lako uhne kaoseong?
en: Do you go to Kaohsiung by High Speed Rail?

ab: teno, la’u nooyo to kuyai no smomacongo ho uhne kaoseong.
en: Yes, I go to Kaohsiung by High Speed Rail.

ab: ho teko uhne thaitong, nenusi na te mayahehe ’o kuyai ho hikoki?
ch: A:你去台東搭火車還是搭飛機?
en: Are you going to Taitung by train or by plane?

ab: te’o nooyo to hikoki, mo mayahehe to kuyai ’o hikoki.
ch: B:我搭飛機，搭飛機比搭火車快。
en: I will take the plane because it is faster than the train.

ab: teko nooyo no vasu hoci eno iachi poʉt’ʉcʉ no kuyai ho teko uhne kiokai?
ch: A:你去教會搭公車還是開車?
en: Are you going to drive to the church or take the bus?

ab: te’o iachi poʉt’ʉcʉ to kuyai ho uhne kiokai, te sonʉhe to vasu.
ch: B:我開車去教會，開車比搭公車方便。
en: I will drive to the church because it is more convenient than the bus.

ab: mo cum’u to emoo suu ’o mo meoi no ’oyonamhia macucuma?
en: Is the market close to your house?

ab: o’a te, la’u yonto ’otovai ho la’u uh to mo meoi no ’oyonamhia macucuma.
ch: B:不近，我都騎摩托車去超市。
en: No, it is not, so I always ride the motorcycle to the market.

ab: mo cum’u to ’oyonatmopsʉ ’o yuuvingkeoku?
en: Is the post office close to the school?

ab: ’a mio c’o, coeconʉ c’o.
en: Yes, it is within walking distance.

ab: mo cum’u to toosva kuyai ’o ’oyonayabobonʉ?
en: Is the restaurant close to the station?

ab: c’o akameosʉ ho te i’mi ta toosva kuyai ho coeconʉ.
ch: B:出車站再走五分鐘就到了。
en: It only takes five minutes after you walk out of the station.
